findings.message: T001 is internally inconsistent: it says the step is "purely additive" and that "no existing files are modified", but the same step also requires moving `track/review-prompts/plan-artifacts.md` into `.harness/custom/review-prompts/`. A move changes the source tree, so this step cannot satisfy both statements as written.

T002 is declared CI-green at the commit boundary, but T003 is where the tests that still assert the old path strings are updated. If those tests are part of `cargo make ci`, the T002 boundary is not actually passable until the T003 expectation changes land too.

CN-01/ADR D5 require the clean-move migration commit to update all references, explicitly including docs, at the same time as loader paths, briefing_file values, harness-policy patterns, and tests. The implementation plan instead makes T002+T003 the atomic migration and then schedules the live-doc reference updates as T004 after that paired migration. That leaves an intermediate planned commit sequence that violates the stated clean-move constraint and contradicts the task coverage that maps CN-01 only to T002/T003 while IN-07/IN-08 are deferred to T004.

OS-01 says the broken `briefing_file` path lint is deferred per the ADR Open Questions, but its ADR reference points to D6. D6 defines `.harness/custom/` ownership and CI behavior, not the broken-path lint deferral, so the rendered spec sends readers to the wrong design rationale for this out-of-scope item.
